text:
Pretty messy. I know its from 2015, but a lot of international collabs lack quality control, mainly on audio and clip sampling. 
Best way to understand a good collab from a messy one is to watch ones like New Years Dreams, international wrestling festival, Octagon, or any YTPMVs from Haiiro_Material. Its just so different once you've seen the full spectrum.

text:
I keep coming back to this collab because this is the first ytpmv collab I watched, so what I thought would be fun is to review each and every part in this collab, so here we go:

Spazza17: What a funny way to start off this collab. Nothing too groundbreaking about your part, but I thought Filthy frank in the background was pretty hilarious. 8/10

Aidan: Ok so this is honestly one of my favorite part in this collab. Smash Bros doesn't get a whole lot of love in the ytpmv community as far as I know, and I love all the melee in-jokes here and how I liked how the S-tier melee characters were used for the sources. No complaints here. 10/10

Marcelo: I have mixed feelings about this part. I think the simple visuals when this part started was bleh, and I know he used the krusty krab pizza source, but it was in a different language, so eh. I mean it gets better from there cause the visuals get better, but honestly, this whole part is pretty eh imo. 7/10

Gurchik: Pretty boring part. I have no idea what that guy was saying in the beginning of his part so I didn't like it at first, then the Rick Astley sentence mixing was kinda a saving grace here a bit for me, cause I love sentence mixing parts like this, but honestly other then that, pretty bland part. 6/10

Jab: Speaking of sentence mixing, this part is a sentence mixing goldmine. I understand Jabs style of his ytpmvs and I like it. They're simple, yet he always uses the best sources. I also have no idea what's going on in the background, but it looks entertaining. Back to the sentence mixing, I LOVE how he used all these songs and mixed it with the source song and it definitely pays off. 9/10

Soul Strike: Ok so here we have a MAD styled part, and the visuals are great! You can tell this guy put a lot of effort in his part and it shows. Nothing else to say here. 10/10

Leo: Another ok part, nothing much to say here other than I really liked how he used the HHgregg source here, and it sounds amazing. I loved how he sings in the style of this song. 8/10

Omni: Ok I am a little confused at Omni's part. Did he make two different ytpmv's for his part? If so I guess I'll talk about 2 in 1 then. 2:17 visuals are alright. That weird cross eyed guy was not fun to look at. 2:26 ok so I like how he used the Lion King as the sources, but it's in Thai or whatever and that kinda disappointed me a bit. Omni's part was alright I guess but it would be better if I understood what they were saying. 7/10

Mirror: This one is interesting. I haven't seen a Jimmy neutron styled ytpmv very often and I am glad to see one. There was a lot going on in this part. The audio from these sources in this part sound a little weird, and I can't understand one word Carl was saying, if he was the one talking or whoever was being sentenced mixed on here. Overall not a bad part, just make the audio sound a little better. 8/10

King: Ok so this is going to sound stupid but the lead source in this part, aka that guy singing, I wanna punch his face lmao. Other than that, the visuals are great. Nothing else to say here. 8/10

Jakob: Ok this part is so hilarious! I love how the whole time he is like "I AM GROOT" that's mixed with the whole song. I also like the visuals here. 10/10

Robyn: Eh, I honestly have nothing to say here cause I have no idea who these guys are or where these sources come from. Visuals are ok tho, although they look pretty boring after a couple of seconds. 7/10

Raphael: I actually made a comment about this part while ago saying how this part kinda ruined the whole vibe of the collab, and I wanna say forget what I said. It was rude and I take back what I said there. I know this whole part is one Brazilian in joke, I don't understand where these come from, therefore I don't really enjoy it as much. I mean the audio sounds good, but mostly the lead source, the weird guy saying "WOOOOOOWOAWOAH" looks very weird and makes the audio sound weird imo, but you know what, honestly when you look past the weird sources, this whole part isn't that bad. 7/10

numb: Ok so I LOVE Undertale and an Undertale related part like this would've got an automatic 10/10 from me but obviously what ruins it is that weird Brazilian des lo funk meme or whatever the fuck it's called on the bottom right and it just seriously ruins everything for me. Like the way these undertale sources are used look and sound really great! Like the audio here is amazing and I like how the Mettaton source sounds with the lead source, and the enemy which I forgot what it was called, I like how it's used as the chorus, but just that ONE awful source on the bottom right ruins everything. 8/10

LENNOZ: Ok so here we have another MAD styled part. Ok here's one thing, the lead source sound, aka that guy screaming is so fucking obnoxious that it makes this part hard to listen to, and it's a shame cause the visuals here are absolutely outstanding, but I think I rather have weird visuals with great audio for parts rather than great visuals with terrible/annoying audio for parts. 7/10

ROB: I like robs part. I think it's great, nothing outstanding or groundbreaking visual wise, but it has great enough visuals for my liking. Other than that, I have no complaints for his part. I also like how he used transformers as the sound effects used in the original song. Very clever. 9/10 

Aluminum: This part is pretty bland visual-wise but I really like this audio. Also what's with the people in this collab using dog dancing we no speak americano in most parts? Not that I have a problem with it cause I find this source very humorous, but it gets annoying seeing the same source over and over again. But this part uses that source pretty well cause I find it hilarious how the freakin dog looked like it was having a seizure. 7/10

Senji: Ok, this is another one of my favorite parts in this collab. Not only is this part clever, but the audio sounds amazing! Like I can't describe how clever and amazing this part is! I love how AVGN screams when the Guitar hero notes trigger and it just looks very well done. Also I LOVE how the meter at the right says "FUCK" like how it shows AVGN's character represented in guitar hero. 10/10

Ketal: Basically what I said about LENNOZ's part can be said here in this part as well. AMAZING visuals, but very annoying sources/audio. I mean I don't like hearing anime girls scream as a lead source and it just really doesn't sit well with me at all. I mean I'll say this though, this part does sound less obnoxious then LENNOZ's part so yeah. 8/10

Carl: When I first watched this collab, I had no idea who that guy singing was, and of course, I couldn't understand a word he said. I know now that that's Edy Lemond and I listened to "Madagascar" and it's an ok song. I like how he is implemented here in this part as well, and seriously bruh? I have no idea who those girls are so but why the nazi outfits lmfao. 9/10

RazQ: Ok here is my third favorite part in this collab. I LOVE how kitchen gun is sentenced mixed here, and it's not like everyone else's "sentence mixing" part in this collab, like it legitimately sounds like he is singing and that just sounds very well done. The visuals are also very well done. Great part! 10/10

SHPIDEN: I don't know what any of these sources are but I guess the visuals and audio are alright. 8/10

Pelz: Holy shit finally the last part lol. Ok so when I first watched this collab, I didn't like this part mainly cause of the sources used here. These sources here creeped me the fuck out lmao, but now I grew some balls and I think this part visual wise is bland, but audio wise sounds hilarious. Ok part in my opinion. 7/10

OMFG this took like an hour to type lmfao, but yeah. Overall, this collab was not terrible at all. No one's parts were terrible or shitty, just some minor nitpicks I had with most parts, but yeah! I appreciate the effort most of these participants put into their parts because I would've BET that this song in general was hard to make a ytpmv out of cause of how fast TTFAF is and how fast some notes are, and I would've bet there was some trouble trying to get the notes right or whatever, but yeah. This collab as a whole to me gets a 8/10. Great collab with some complaints, but nothing too bad!
author: @Pancakedoezgaming21

text:
I am going to respond to two of these criticisms.
numb:He used a very old Spanish meme song that I believe is the origin of the Me Gusta reaction meme, popular around 2013 if I recall. This should not have detracted since it's so damn miniscule compared to the rest of the sources. I'm actually more angry you mistook Spanish with Portuguese, they're quite different languages. I'm not too mad though.
Carl:I think those girls are from the anime Girls und Panzer. The word mixing was good enough to me, although I'm slightly better than average picking out sounds in chaos.

author: @TriforceOfDiarrhea

text:
+EpicRemix 0_o These collabs are generally private, with no announcement video. It's to ensure all the entries aren't made by random no-name shitty vid makers.

I'll help ya out though buddy.

Steps on how to get into a ytpmv collab:

Step 1) Become likable
Step 2) Make ytpmv friends
Step 3) Have at least 4 good ytpmvs on your channel. No less. Every sparta counts for -1 ytpmvs.
Step 4) Download at least one of these two programs: Reaper, After Effects
Step 5) This next step is kinda tricky. You're going to need to become a youtube partner on a seperate account. This can take a while. I would suggest making a top 10 channel. That's what a lot of notable ytpmvers did.
Step 6) Fund your youtube partner money into ytpmv lessons. (Don't cheap out on these. I have a fair rate, but the more expensive the better. Xarlable charges $100 per 30 min lesson.
Step 7) The last step is to become a notable ytpmver. You can do this in a couple ways. The more honourable way would of course be to make plenty of good quality vids, but you can also take the pelz route and make tons of in-joke vids that no one gets.
Step 8) Get invited to a collab!

You haven't finished step 1.
author: @TriforceOfDiarrhea

text: +Gojira Godzilla Godzira Gozira Zilla I mean, there are usually bad parts in collabs who are, you know, the sentencing ironic shit, who are actually entertaining, or the so bad things who are good somehow, and makes the collaboration unique and fun, but there isn't any in this one.

text:
+MrSpazzaneve17 Yeah, i totally understand you guys were busy etc, but why do everyone says in the comments this is the "best collab ever" and shit like that.
Also i don't understand why you talk about my collab, i didn't make all the parts, so if the visuals are better than the audio, it's not because of me but because of the participants, they weren't asked to make better visuals than audio. And to be honest, i don't understand why you didn't postpone the deadline in that case. A dead collab is still better than a bad collab.

Also to everyone, imo sound is as important as visuals, but there, visuals and sounds are both boring. I'm totally okay with boxvisuals, but not in a 7 minutes long collab, seriously.
author: @BaslesPad
